lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Woooo wooo wooooo woooo

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Take pity on me, please - eternity stretches out before me and I am helpless in its grasp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why, what is the matter?

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry - I was just wailing out loud. I didn't mean to scare you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, that's okay - it takes more than a ghost to scare me. What is wrong?

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Do you know the history of our town?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I do. It is a very sad story.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Would you help us obtain our release into the next world?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No - could you tell me?

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Do you know why ghosts exist?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A ghost is a soul left in limbo, unable to pass over to the next world; they might have something left to do in this world that torments them, or they might just have died in a state of confusion.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Yes, that is normally the case. But here in Port Phasmatys, we of this town once chose freely to become ghosts!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why on earth would you do such a thing?

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: It is a long story. Many years ago, this was a thriving port, a trading centre to the eastern lands of Gielinor. We became rich on the profits made from the traders that came across the eastern seas.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: We were very happy... until Lord Drakan noticed us.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: He sent unholy creatures to demand that a blood-tithe be paid to the Lord Vampyre, as is required from all in the domain of Morytania. We had no choice but to agree to his demands.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: As the years went by, our numbers dwindled and many spoke of abandoning the town for safer lands. Then, Necrovarus came to us.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: He came from the eastern lands, but of more than that, little is known. Some say he was once a mage, some say a priest. Either way, he was in possession of knowledge totally unknown to even the most learned among us.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Necrovarus told us that he had been brought by a vision he'd had of an underground source of power. He inspired us to delve beneath the town, promising us the power to repel the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Deep underneath Phasmatys, we found a pool of green slime that Necrovarus called ectoplasm. He showed us how to build the Ectofuntus, which would turn the ectoplasm into the power he had promised us.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Indeed, this Ectopower did repel the vampyres; they would not enter Phasmatys once the Ectofuntus began working. But little did we know that we had exchanged one evil for yet another - Ectopower.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Little by little, we began to lose any desire for food or water, and our desire for the Ectopower grew until it dominated our thoughts entirely. Our bodies shrivelled and, one by one, we died.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: The Ectofuntus and the power it emanates keeps us here as ghosts - some, like myself, content to remain in this world; some becoming tortured souls who we do not allow to pass our gates.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: We would be able to pass over into the next world but Necrovarus has used his power to create a psychic barrier, preventing us.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: We must remain here for all eternity, even unto the very end of the world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a very sad story.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Would you help us obtain our release into the next world?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, of course I will. Tell me what you want me to do.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Oh, thank you!

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Necrovarus will not listen to those of us who are already dead. He might rethink his position if someone with a mortal soul pleaded our cause.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: If he declines, there may yet be another way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, but it isn't really my problem.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: No, you're right - it's our own fault. If you do change your mind though, please come back and help us. We will be here ... forever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Leave me alone - I'm scared of ghosts!!!

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: I sense that you have not yet spoken to Necrovarus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I was just getting to that.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Well, I suppose we do have all the time in the world...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please, listen to me-

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: No - listen to me. Go from this place and do not return, or I will remove your head.

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: Woooo wooo wooooo woooo

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must speak with you on behalf of Velorina.

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: You dare to speak that name in this place?????

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She wants to pass-

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: Silence!! Or I will incinerate the flesh from your bones!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But she-

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: Get out of my sight!! Or I promise you that you will regret your insolence for the rest of eternity!!!

sections.title: Returning to Velorina after talking to Necrovarus

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, but Necrovarus will not let you go.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: I feared as much. His spirit is a thing of fire and wrath.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You spoke of another way.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: It is only a small chance. During the building of the Ectofuntus one of Necrovarus's disciples spoke out against him. It is such a long time ago I cannot remember her name, although I knew her as a friend.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: She fled before the Ectofuntus took control over us, but being a disciple of Necrovarus she would have been privy to many of his darkest secrets. She may know of a way to aid us without Necrovarus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where this woman can be found?

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: I have a vision of a small wooden shack, the land it was built on the unholy soil of Morytania. I sense the sea is very close, and that there are looms castles to the west and the east.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If it was such a long time ago, won't she be dead already?

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: She was a friend of mine. Had she died, I would have felt her spirit pass over to the next world, though I may not follow.

sections.title: Talking to the Old crone again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am afraid I have not found your son yet.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: I never expected that you would find him - although if you do, please let me know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Were you once a disciple of Necrovarus in the Temple of Phasmatys, old woman?

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: I don't remember; I am so old and my memory goes back only so far...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything that can help to refresh your memory?

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Yes, I would love a nice hot cup of nettle tea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's some tea for you, like you asked.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Yes, but it's not in my special cup! I only ever drink from my special cup!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Can I have this special cup, then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where I can find nettles around here?

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: I believe they grow wild in the Haunted Forest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's a lovely cup of tea for you, in your own special cup.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Oh no, it hasn't got milk in it. I only drink tea with milk, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's a lovely cup of milky tea for you, in your own special cup.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Ah, that's better. Now, let me see... Yes, I was once a disciple of Necrovarus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have come from Velorina. She needs your help.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Velorina? That name sounds familiar...

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Yes, Velorina was once a very good friend! It has been many years since we spoke last. How is she?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She's a ghost, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: They are all dead, then. Even Velorina. I should have done something to stop what was happening, instead of running away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She and many others want to pass on into the next world, but Necrovarus will not let them. Do you know of any way to help them?

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: There might be one way...

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Do you have such a thing as a ghostspeak amulet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No I don't, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Well, you'll need to find one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm carrying one in my bag.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Well, that's a stroke of luck.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'm wearing one right now.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Well, that's a stroke of luck.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: There is an enchantment that I can perform on such an amulet that will give it the power of command over ghosts. It will work only once, but it will enable you to command Necrovarus to let the ghosts pass on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need to perform the enchantment?

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Necrovarus had a magical robe for which he must have no more use. Only these robes hold the power needed to perform the enchantment.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: All his rituals came from a book written by an ancient sorcerer from the East called Haricanto. Bring me this strange book.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You are doing so much for me - is there anything I can do for you?

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: I have lived here on my own for many years, but not always. When I left Port Phasmatys, I took my son with me. He grew up to be a fine boy, always in love with the sea.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: He was about twelve years old when he ran away with some pirates to be a cabin boy. I never saw him again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's the second saddest story I have heard today.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: If you ever see him, please give him this...and tell him that his mother still loves him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Was this his boat?

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Yes, he made it himself. It is a model of the very ship in which he sailed away. The paint has peeled off and it has lost its flag, but I could never throw it away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I find him, I will pass it on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Remind me - what can I do about Necrovarus?

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Do you have such a thing as a ghostspeak amulet?

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: There is an enchantment that I can perform on such an amulet that will give it the power of command over ghosts. It will work only once, but it will enable you to command Necrovarus to let the ghosts pass on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need to perform the enchantment?

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Necrovarus had a magical robe for which he must have no more use. Only these robes hold the power needed to perform the enchantment.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: All his rituals came from a book written by an ancient sorcerer from the East called Haricanto. Bring me this strange book.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: I cannot read the strange letters of the eastern lands. I will need something to help me translate the book.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you want me to get for you?

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Necrovarus had a magical robe for which he must have no more use. Only these robes hold the power needed to perform the enchantment.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: All his rituals came from a book written by an ancient sorcerer from the East called Haricanto. Bring me this strange book.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: I cannot read the strange letters of the eastern lands. I will need something to help me translate the book.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and search for those items you need.

sections.title: Talking to Velorina after talking to the Old crone

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where I can find the Book of Haricanto?

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Nobody knows what has happened to the Book. It was stolen when our port was raided by pirates many years ago, and never seen since.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know where I can find the Robes of Necrovarus?

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: I imagine they are still worn by his mortal body, which now lies in a coffin inside the Temple.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need something to translate the Book of Haricanto.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: I don't really know. You could try asking some of the traders from the East - they might be able to help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is your name?

lines.speaker: Old man
lines.text: I don't remember. Everyone around here just calls me 'boy'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're the cabin boy?!?

lines.speaker: Old man
lines.text: Yes, and proud of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have the key to the chest?

lines.speaker: Old man
lines.text: Hang on, let me ask the Captain ...

lines.speaker: Old man
lines.text: The Captain says no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this your toy boat?

lines.speaker: Old man
lines.text: No - I made a toy boat a long while ago, but that one had a flag.

lines.speaker: Old man
lines.text: No - I made a toy boat a long while ago, but the colours on the flag were different.

lines.speaker: Old man
lines.text: My word - so it is!!! I never thought I would see it again!! Where did you get it from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Your mother gave it to me to pass on to you.

lines.speaker: Old man
lines.text: My mother? She still lives?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, in a shack to the west of here.

lines.speaker: Old man
lines.text: After all these years ...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have the key to the chest, then?

lines.speaker: Old man
lines.text: Hang on, let me ask the Captain ...

lines.speaker: Old man
lines.text: The Captain says yes.

sections.title: Talking to the Old man again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How is it going?

lines.speaker: Old man
lines.text: Wonderful, wonderful! Mother's coming to get me!

sections.title: Talking to the Old crone after finding the old man

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good news! I have found your son!

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Goodness! Where is he?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He lives on a shipwreck to the east of here. He remembers you and wishes you well.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Oh thank you!! I will travel to see him as soon as I am able!!

lines.speaker: Ghost captain
lines.text: Woooo wooo wooooo woooo

lines.speaker: Ghost captain
lines.text: Would you like to visit Dragontooth Island? It is a most pleasant island in the sea between Morytania and the lands of the east. It'll cost you 25 ectotokens for the return trip.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, here's 25 ectotokens. Let's go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I pay 500 ectotokens, can I get unlimited trips?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If I pay 500 ectotokens, can I get unlimited trips?

lines.speaker: Ghost captain
lines.text: Yes, that seems fair. Okay, hand them over and we can set off.

lines.speaker: Ghost captain
lines.text: Yes, I'd accept that deal. But as you don't have enough ectotokens for that, would you like to pay 25 ectotokens for a trip now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not now, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not now, thanks.

sections.title: Something to translate the Book of Haricanto

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you got an oak longbow that I can get Robin to sign for you?

lines.speaker: Ak-Haranu
lines.text: No, Ak-Haranu afraid that no longbow in supply at moment. You must make one or buy one.

lines.speaker: Ak-Haranu
lines.text: How Master [player name] getting on with longbow?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll let you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's nice to see a human face around here.

lines.speaker: Ak-Haranu
lines.text: My name Ak-Haranu. I am trader, come from many far across sea in east.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You come from the lands of the East? Do you have anything that can help me translate a book that is scribed in your language?

lines.speaker: Ak-Haranu
lines.text: Ak-Haranu may help you. A translation manual I have, much good for reading Eastern language.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much do you want for it?

lines.speaker: Ak-Haranu
lines.text: Ak-Haranu not want money for this book, as is such small thing. But there may be something you could do for me. I am big admirer of Robin, Master Bowman. He staying in village inn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What would you like me to do?

lines.speaker: Ak-Haranu
lines.text: Please get Master Bowman sign an oak longbow for me, so Ak-Haranu can show family and friends when returning home and become much admired. Then I give you book in exchange.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, wait here - I'll get you your bow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, I have too much to do at the moment.

sections.title: Talking to Robin after getting the bow signed

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, still here then?

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: Do you want another game of Runedraw?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: Hey you, why don't you have a game of Runedraw with me? If you've got 25 gold pieces I've got a bag of runes we can use.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have enough money on me for a game of Runedraw at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'll give you a game.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So are you going to pay up then? You owe me [#] gold coins!

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: How about another game? I'll pay you back with the winnings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if you lose again?

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: Er ... we'll deal with that when we come to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, you didn't pay up the last time you lost.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well done, you beat me. But you still owe me [#] gold pieces.

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: Err ... how about another game? We can settle up after we've finished playing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've had enough of you not paying up - you owe me 100 gold coins. I'm going to tell the ghosts what you're doing.

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: Please don't do that!!! They will suck the life from my bones!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about you signing my longbow then?

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: Yes, anything!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you play Runedraw?

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: Two players take turns to draw a rune from a bag, which contains ten runes in total. Each rune has a different value: an air rune is worth one point, up to a Nature rune which is worth nine points.

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: If a player draws the Death rune then the game is over, and they have lost.

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: A player can choose to hold if they wish and not draw any more runes, but this runs the risk of the other player drawing more runes until they have a greater points total and win.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, that doesn't sound fun.

sections.title: Talking to Ak-Haranu after getting the bow signed

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, I seem to have lost the translation manual you gave me.

lines.speaker: Ak-Haranu
lines.text: Ah, no worry, my friend. For the gift of longbow Ak-Haranu would give a thousand books.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you for the translation manual, Ak-Haranu - it may save many souls before long.

lines.speaker: Ak-Haranu
lines.text: And Ak-Haranu thanks you for kind gift of longbow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have your signed longbow for you.

lines.speaker: Ak-Haranu
lines.text: Ah, can it be true? You have obtained bow from Master Bowman?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He was more than happy to oblige (cough). Here you are.

lines.speaker: Ak-Haranu
lines.text: May honour be bestowed upon you and your family!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, I seem to have lost my petition form.

lines.speaker: Gravingas
lines.text: Blown away in the sea breeze, hey? Oh well, can't be helped. I'd give you another one, but you look like you're carrying too much already.

lines.speaker: Gravingas
lines.text: Blown away in the sea breeze, hey? Oh well, can't be helped. Here's another one, but you'll have to start from scratch again.

lines.speaker: Gravingas
lines.text: Come on - you haven't even started yet! You need 10 more signatures.

lines.speaker: Gravingas
lines.text: Well, that's a start I suppose You need [#] more signatures.

lines.speaker: Gravingas
lines.text: Not doing bad I see! You need 6 more signatures.

lines.speaker: Gravingas
lines.text: Are you sure you haven't done this type of thing before? You need 3 more signatures.

lines.speaker: Gravingas
lines.text: Very nearly there!! Only 1 more signature to go.

lines.speaker: Gravingas
lines.text: You've got them all! Now go and present it to Necrovarus!!

lines.speaker: Gravingas
lines.text: Woooo wooo wooooo woooo

lines.speaker: Gravingas
lines.text: Will you join with me and protest against the evil desires of Necrovarus and his disciples?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: After hearing Velorina's story I will be happy to help out.

lines.speaker: Gravingas
lines.text: I'd give you a petition form, but you look like you're carrying too much already.

lines.speaker: Gravingas
lines.text: Excellent, excellent!! Here - take this petition form, and try and get 10 signatures from the townsfolk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, I don't really think I should get involved.

sections.title: Talking to Robin with the innkeeper's task

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've brought you a clean bedsheet, Robin.

lines.speaker: Robin
lines.text: Well it's about time. Run along now, I need some Robin time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, still here then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: Greetings, traveller. Welcome to 'The Green Ghost' Tavern. What can I do you for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I buy a beer?

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: Sorry, but our pumps dried up over half a century ago. We of this village do not have much of a thirst these days.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I hear some gossip?

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: I suppose, as long as you keep it to yourself...

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: You see Gravingas out there in the marketplace? He speaks for the silent majority of Port Phasmatys, for those of us who would prefer to pass over into the next world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to the folk of this town?

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: That's a long story, my friend, but you look like you have the time...

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: Do you know why ghosts exist?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A ghost is a soul left in limbo, unable to pass over to the next world; they might have something left to do in this world that torments them, or they might just have died in a state of confusion.

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: Yes, that is normally the case. But here in Port Phasmatys, we of this town once chose freely to become ghosts!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why on earth would you do such a thing?

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: It is a long story. Many years ago, this was a thriving port, a trading centre to the eastern lands of Gielinor. We became rich on the profits made from the traders that came across the eastern seas.

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: We were very happy... until Lord Drakan noticed us.

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: He sent unholy creatures to demand that a blood-tithe be paid to the Lord Vampyre, as is required from all in the domain of Morytania. We had no choice but to agree to his demands.

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: As the years went by, our numbers dwindled and many spoke of abandoning the town for safer lands. Then, Necrovarus came to us.

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: He came from the eastern lands, but of more than that, little is known. Some say he was once a mage, some say a priest. Either way, he was in possession of knowledge totally unknown to even the most learned among us.

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: Necrovarus told us that he had been brought by a vision he'd had of an underground source of power. He inspired us to delve beneath the town, promising us the power to repel the vampyres.

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: Deep underneath Phasmatys, we found a pool of green slime that Necrovarus called ectoplasm. He showed us how to build the Ectofuntus, which would turn the ectoplasm into the power he had promised us.

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: Indeed, this Ectopower did repel the vampyres; they would not enter Phasmatys once the Ectofuntus began working. But little did we know that we had exchanged one evil for yet another - Ectopower.

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: Little by little, we began to lose any desire for food or water, and our desire for the Ectopower grew until it dominated our thoughts entirely. Our bodies shrivelled and, one by one, we died.

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: The Ectofuntus and the power it emanates keeps us here as ghosts - some, like myself, content to remain in this world; some becoming tortured souls who we do not allow to pass our gates.

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: We would be able to pass over into the next world but Necrovarus has used his power to create a psychic barrier, preventing us.

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: We must remain here for all eternity, even unto the very end of the world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any jobs I can do?

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: Yes, actually, I do. We have a very famous Master Bowman named Robin staying with us at the moment. Could you take him some clean bed linen for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I'd be delighted.

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: Oh, thank you. Be careful with that Robin, though - he's far too full of himself, that one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I didn't mean a job like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any jobs I can do?

lines.speaker: Ghost innkeeper
lines.text: Well, you could take that bedsheet through to Robin like I asked.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing, thanks.

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: Woooo wooo wooooo woooo

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you sign this petition form, please?

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: I'm sorry, but it's hard to believe that a mortal could be interested in helping us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you sign this petition form, please?

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: Why are you wearing that bedsheet? If you're trying to pretend to be one of us, you're not fooling anybody - you're not even green!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Would you sign this petition form, please?

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: Most certainly, I will.

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: I'll do anything that annoys Necrovarus.

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: Yes! It's about time somebody did something about Necrovarus.

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: Yes, of course.

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: It'll cost you...

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: I will if you make it worth my while...

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: You scratch my back and I'll scratch yours...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much?

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: Oh, it'll cost you [1-3] ecto-tokens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have that many on me.

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: No tokens, no signature.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, if you insist.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's no way I'm giving in to corruption.

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: Suit yourself.

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: I will have you know that I am a fervent supporter of Necrovarus.

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: How dare you accost me in the street?

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: Get lost.

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: I don't have time for this nonsense.

lines.speaker: Ghost villager
lines.text: You only just asked me the same thing! Leave me alone - I've had my say!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you for your kind support.

sections.title: Talking to Necrovarus whilst collecting signatures

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, have you changed your mind yet?

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: NEVER!!!!!

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: WHICH IS A VERY LONG TIME!!!!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It matters not that you ignore your citizens' wishes, Necrovarus. Wheels have been set in motion, wheels that will set them free.

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: I have almost completely lost patience with you, mortal. Another word, and every threat I have uttered will be made real for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I must let you know, Necrovarus, that I am collecting signatures from the citizens of Port Phasmatys.

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: Oh, I do admire an activist. Do let me know when you're finished, and I'll give it my fullest consideration.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Necrovarus, I am presenting you with a petition form that has been signed by 10 citizens of Port Phasmatys.

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: A petition you say? Continue, mortal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It says that the citizens of Port Phasmatys should have the right to choose whether they pass over into the next world or not, and not have this decided by the powers that be on their behalf.

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: I see.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you will let them pass over if they wish?

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: Oh yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: NO!!!!! Get out of my sight before I burn every ounce of flesh from your bones!!!!!

lines.speaker: Ghost disciple
lines.text: What are you doing going in there?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Err, I was just curious...

lines.speaker: Ghost disciple
lines.text: Inside that room is a coffin, inside which lie the mortal remains of our most glorious master, Necrovarus. None may enter.

sections.title: Talking to the Old crone with the materials
Talking to the Old crone again

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Did it work?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I haven't tried it yet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seem to have lost my ghostspeak amulet.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Oh dear, that's unfortunate. You'll have to go and find another one, then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have something for you.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: The Book of Haricanto! I have no idea how you came by this, but well done!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have something for you.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: A translation manual - yes, this should do the job.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have something for you.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Good - the robes of Necrovarus.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Wonderful; that's everything I need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seem to have lost my ghostspeak amulet.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: Oh dear, that's unfortunate. You'll have to go and find another one, then.

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: I will now perform the ritual of enchantment.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: How is it going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have had the Amulet of Ghostspeak enchanted, which I shall use to command Necrovarus to set you free.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Oh, kind adventurer - you are the answer to all our prayers!

sections.title: Talking to Necrovarus after freeing Port Phasmatys

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Told you I'd defeat you, Necrovarus. My advice to you is to pass over to the next world yourself with everybody else.

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: I should fry you for what you have done...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Quiet, evil priest!! If you try anything I will command you again, but this time it will be to throw yourself into the Endless Void for the rest of eternity.

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: Please no! I will do whatever you say!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It matters not that you ignore your citizens' wishes, Necrovarus. Wheels have been set in motion, wheels that will set them free.

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: I have almost completely lost patience with you, mortal. Another word, and every threat I have uttered will be made real for you.

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: You dare to face me again - you must be truly insane!!!!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, Necrovarus, I am not insane. With this enchanted amulet of ghostspeak I have the power to command you to do my will!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let any ghost who so wishes pass on into the next world.

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: I - will - let ...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Carry on...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: ... ghost who so wishes ...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think we're almost getting there...

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: ... pass into the next world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me a joke.

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: Knock knock

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who's there?

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: Egbert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Egbert who?

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: Egbert no bacon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do a chicken impression.

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: Cluck cluck squuuaaaakkkk cluck cluck

lines.speaker: Necrovarus
lines.text: I think I've laid an egg...

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: You don't need to tell me [player name] - I sensed the removal of Necrovarus's psychic barrier!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Only happy to help out.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Here, take this as a thank you for the service that you have given us.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: This is an Ectophial. If you ever want to come back to Port Phasmatys, empty this on the floor beneath your feet, and you will be instantly teleported to the temple - the source of its power.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Remember that once the Ectophial has been used you need to refill it from the Ectofuntus.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought you were going to pass over to the next world.

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: All in good time, [player name]. We stand forever in your debt, and will certainly put in a good word for you when we pass over.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have another Ectophial?

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Really? You've already got an Ectophial.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know, but can I have another one anyway?

lines.speaker: Velorina
lines.text: Of course you can, you have helped us more than we could ever have hoped.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have I? Okay, forget it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why are you still protesting? I have commanded Necrovarus to let you and your friends do as you like! There's no need for this anymore!

lines.speaker: Gravingas
lines.text: There's always a need for a healthy interest in politics.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, suit yourself then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good news - the enchantment worked!

lines.speaker: Old crone
lines.text: So Necrovarus has let the ghosts go free.... You have achieved many wonderful things, my young friend.
